name: Television
description:
'''Television''' was an American [[rock music|rock]] band formed in [[New York City]] in 1973. The group's most prominent lineup consisted of [[Tom Verlaine]] (vocals, guitar), [[Richard Lloyd (guitarist)|Richard Lloyd]] (guitar), [[Billy Ficca]] (drums), and [[Fred Smith (bassist)|Fred Smith]] (bass). An early fixture of [[CBGB]] and the [[Punk rock#New York City|1970s New York rock scene]], the band is considered influential in the development of [[punk rock|punk]] and [[alternative rock]].{{sfn|Simpson|2013}}

Although they recorded in a stripped-down, [[guitar]]-based manner similar to their punk contemporaries, Television's music was by comparison clean, improvisational, and technically proficient, drawing influence from [[jazz]] and 1960s rock.{{sfn|Schinder|Schwartz|2008|page=541}} The group's 1977 debut album, ''[[Marquee Moon]]'', is considered one of the defining releases of the punk era. The group released another album the following year titled ''[[Adventure (Television album)|Adventure]]'', after which they disbanded, but reunited in 1992 to record a [[Television (Television album)|self-titled album]]. Television disbanded again two years later, but they began occasionally performing live in 2001, continuing until 2023 when Verlaine died.
